Anemia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the amount of hemoglobin in 100 ml of blood.
Anemia.Grade 3: Hgb <8.0 g/dL; <4.9 mmol/L; <80 g/L; transfusion indicated

Thrombocytopenia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in platelets in the blood.

Neutropenia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in neutrophils in the blood.

Febrile neutropenia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in neutrophils associated with fever.
Febrile neutropenia.Grade 3: ANC <1000/mm3 with a single temperature of >38.3 degrees C (101 degrees F) or a sustained temperature of >=38 degrees C (100.4 degrees F) for more than one hour

Lymphopenia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in the number of lymphocytes in the blood.
